message:
feat(simulation): tick 202 execute_until_with_cx inject via MockWezterm::inject_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Scenario::execute_until_with_cx threaded cx through the per-event
pre-injection checkpoint but then called the legacy ambient
`mock.inject(pane, event)` which uses `self.panes.write().await` —
a plain RwLock acquire that picks up cx via `Cx::current()`
thread-local fallback.

`MockWezterm::inject_with_cx` (wezterm.rs L6815, added in an earlier
tick) routes the RwLock acquire through `write_with_cx(cx)` so the
caller's explicit cx bounds the write-lock wait.

Tick 202 switches L745 to `mock.inject_with_cx(cx, pane, event)`.
A long-held write lock (from concurrent simulation harnesses) plus
an outer cx-cancel now deterministically interrupts the write-lock
wait instead of inheriting whatever thread-local cx happens to be
installed.

Legacy Scenario::execute_until (L701) and the resize-timeline
variant (L781 / L968 wrapper) are unchanged — the timeline variant
intentionally delegates to the legacy path so nanosecond-scale
telemetry remains consistent.

Verification: 185 / 185 simulation:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
